channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Airbnb is offering temporary housing for people impacted by the L.A. wildfires
item.description: Airbnb’s nonprofit arm, Airbnb.org, is providing free, temporary housing for Los Angeles residents who have been displaced or forced to evacuate due to the ongoing wildfires. “To support residents who have been displaced from their homes or forced to evacuate by the wildfires, Airbnb.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The Evolution of Home Office Essentials in 2025
item.description: Discover the latest tools and technologies transforming the world of remote work, from Samsung's Smart Monitor M9 to Netgear's Orbi 870 WiFi router.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the globe, at least 4 in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Why I’m making 2025 my year of dead-tree books
item.description: Over the past 17 years or so, I have happily paid for hundreds of e-books—everything from reference works to history to novels to comics. Having immediate access to all of them on a device that fits in my pocket ranks among my favorite miracles of the digital age.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn’t just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cisco in 2025: Lots of hard work ahead
item.description: For a company of its size, Cisco executed a fairly short list of technology rollouts in 2024 and made a surprising lack of major updates to its core networking portfolio, which might have contributed to the 23% decline in networking revenue Cisco reported in its latest quarterly financials.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Federal cyber leaders on delivering innovation
item.description: Need innovation inspiration when it comes to cyber? The post Federal cyber leaders on delivering innovation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Ebay Listings Coming to Facebook Marketplace - A New Partnership for Shoppers and Sellers
item.description: Meta announces partnership with eBay, allowing users to view listings on Facebook Marketplace, sparking hopes of increased competition and exposure for both platforms.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Website certificates that expire every six weeks? What IT should know
item.description: Industry forces — led by Apple and Google — are pushing for a sharp acceleration of how often website certificates must be updated, but the stated security reason is raising an awful lot of eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Port strike looms ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Tens of thousands of longshoremen could walk off the job from Maine to Texas next week if their union and management can’t resolve long-running contract issues.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How adding capacity to a network could reduce IT costs
item.description: Finding an enterprise looking to spend more on network equipment is like finding gold while digging for worms for bait. Cost management used to be an option in enhancing profits, but these days it’s a mantra.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia’s Project DIGITS puts AI supercomputing chips on the desktop
item.description: Nvidia has built its generative AI (genAI) business on delivering massive computing capacity to data centers where it can be used to train and refine large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NASA's Martian helicopter promises unprecedented views of the red planet
item.description: NASA's Ingenuity helicopter, which flew on Mars in April 2021, became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on a planet other than Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities
item.description: The Treasury hack is sparking new questions about how agencies ensure the security of third-party technology service providers. The post Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation
